name: tier-enforcement-status
question: Does every domain-write handler under crates/feedbackmonk-api/src/handlers/ either consult check_tier_quota() before its first write OR appear in the allowlist? Does tier_quotas() return the Contract C19 canonical shape per Tier variant? (With --full: do the end-to-end cap-firing smoke tests pass?)

output.schema: PASS or FAIL <n> with probe-level offender detail. Exit 0 on PASS, 1 on FAIL, 2 on environment failure.

consultation.typical_sessions_using: every-commit-during-P3+ (touching handlers/, tier model, or tier_quota repo)

consumer_scope: P3+ all crates (every commit touching feedbackmonk-api/handlers/, feedbackmonk-core/src/tier.rs, or feedbackmonk-repository/src/tier_quota.rs; CI gate from P3 start)
fallback: Probe A: grep every pub async fn in crates/feedbackmonk-api/src/handlers/*.rs for a write-pattern (\.create\(, \.submit_*, \.update_*); confirm each either calls check_tier_quota before the write or is in allowlist.toml. Probe B: read crates/feedbackmonk-core/src/tier.rs and confirm tier_quotas() match arms for Free/Starter/Pro/SelfHost carry the Contract C19 token set. Probe C: cargo test --test tier_enforcement_smoke -p feedbackmonk-api with dev Postgres up.

provenance.authored_by: P3 Stage 1 worker (Task Zero — orchestrated session-20260514-102233-006)
provenance.rationale: FR-FBR-14 (tier enforcement + caps + footer) + DEC-FBR-03 (pricing tier matrix). Three-leg defense per P3 plan § Testability Gate: (1) Tier enum + TierQuotas type-system; (2) THIS oracle (handler-coverage + config-shape + integration smoke); (3) sqlx::test fixtures driving HTTP cap-firing paths. Cold-start vacuous-PASS supports Task Zero order-of-operations — built BEFORE Phase 4 cap-wiring so every subsequent commit closes its develop/test/fix loop against the oracle.
